Iron prices have doubled since the road south closed
A merchant caravan turned back before reaching the pass
Nobody's buying grain anymore, and no one will say why
The last three shipments of salt never arrived
A peddler was selling swords out of his cart, good ones, far too cheap
The wool buyers from the east haven't shown up this season
Someone bought every lantern and oil flask the general store had in one visit
The ferry operator is charging triple and claims it's not his choice
A trader offered to pay in foreign coin no one here recognized
The lumber mill cut its output in half but won't explain the reason
Herb prices spiked overnight after a stranger visited the apothecary
Three merchants pooled their guards for the trip north, which they've never done before
The tanner's been stockpiling leather instead of selling, says he's got a private buyer
No one's seen the usual tax collector in weeks, and a new one showed up asking odd questions
A whole wagon of tools arrived with no buyer's mark and no one claimed it
The blacksmith turned down a commission for the first time anyone can remember
The spice merchant says her suppliers have gone quiet, every single one
Someone's been buying up all the rope and chain in the area
The baker raised prices and muttered something about flour shipments stopping
A caravan guard quit on the spot rather than take the western route
The fishmonger's been trading for arrows instead of coin
Two competing merchants suddenly started cooperating, which has people nervous
An entire barge of goods was turned away at the river landing, no reason given
The cloth dyer packed up shop overnight and left town without a word
Someone's offering premium coin for maps of the old roads, no questions asked
Candle wax has gotten so expensive that people are going back to tallow dips
The leather merchant doubled her order this month and won't say who it's for
A tinker came through selling tools at half price and every single one was stamped with a mark nobody recognized
The cooper ran out of barrel staves and says his timber supplier stopped delivering without notice
The general store started rationing nails and hinges, first time in years
A merchant from the east is offering triple the usual rate for medicinal herbs, dried or fresh
The pottery shop has been turning away custom orders, says they can't get the right clay anymore
A wagon loaded with furs came through headed the wrong direction for the season
The chandler is melting down old candles to recast them, says new tallow is impossible to source
Someone paid the stablemaster in gemstones for a horse and left before anyone could ask questions
The salt trader arrived with an armed escort for the first time anyone can remember
A merchant's scales were found to be off by a quarter weight and nobody knows if it was intentional
The wool market has collapsed: buyers sitting on full stores while sellers can't move their clip
A stranger bought the entire stock of dried meat from the butcher and left town the same day
The weaver says linen thread has tripled in price because the flax harvest failed somewhere upstream
A shipment of dye arrived spoiled and the merchant is blaming the shipper, who blames the roads
The farrier is turning away work because he can't get quality iron anymore
Someone ordered two hundred arrows and paid in advance, but never came to collect them
The grain broker has been meeting with people after hours and the transactions aren't going through the market book
A caravan that usually takes four days arrived in eight, and the drivers won't talk about the delay
The glassmaker closed his kiln for a week and said it was maintenance, but he was seen packing crates
Pitch and tar prices spiked after someone bought the whole supply at the river dock
The fishermen are selling their catch to a buyer from out of town instead of the local market
A peddler is buying old tools, broken or not, and paying more than they're worth
The mill is grinding day and night, but the flour isn't showing up at the baker's
A merchant was seen paying off a debt with goods instead of coin, which nobody does unless coin is short
The herbalist is growing things she doesn't usually sell and won't say who placed the order
A trade factor from a distant settlement set up in the tavern and has been interviewing craftspeople one by one
The charcoal supply dried up in a week and the smiths are burning through their reserves
A barge captain says the river toll went up at every checkpoint this month
The wool merchant is buying raw fleece at prices that don't make sense unless she knows something the rest of us don't
A jewelry maker packed up her tools and moved her entire workshop without telling anyone where she was going
The tavern stopped serving wine and switched to ale, says wine shipments have been unreliable for months
A trader arrived with a cartload of weapons and said he'd sell only to the settlement council, not individuals
The rope maker says demand has outpaced his supply for three straight months and he can't keep up
Someone is stockpiling firewood far beyond what a single household needs
The pottery merchant from the coast hasn't been seen in two seasons and nobody has taken over his route
A tanner from out of town set up just outside the gates and is undercutting the local tanner by half
The baker's been experimenting with different grains because wheat is getting harder to source
A cart of mining equipment came through headed into the hills, but there are no active claims up there
The lamp oil is getting cut with something: burns dirtier and doesn't last as long
A merchant tried to exchange a large quantity of foreign silver and the money changer refused the rate
The butcher is buying livestock from much farther away than usual and says the local herds are spoken for
Someone placed a standing order for bandages and splints with the weaver: a large quantity, recurring
The smith has been working after hours with the doors shut, and the work isn't on the order board
A trade convoy that was expected a month ago still hasn't arrived, and the outpost it departed from says it left on time
The old trade road nobody uses anymore has fresh wheel ruts on it
A merchant confided that her insurance costs tripled because the underwriters consider the route too dangerous now
The dyer is buying mordants in bulk from three different suppliers, which usually means a large commission from someone with money
Timber prices dropped suddenly and nobody can figure out where the surplus is coming from
The stablemaster is selling horses below cost to anyone heading east, says he'd rather lose the money than the animals
A scribe set up in the market offering to copy documents for free, and people are suspicious of his motives
The local ale has gotten worse and the brewer blames the water, but nobody else has noticed a change in the well
A cloth merchant arrived with fabrics nobody here can afford and seemed surprised there wasn't a wealthier clientele
The courier service between here and the coast has been reduced to once a month, down from weekly
A merchant's warehouse was found nearly empty and he claims he moved the goods, but nobody saw a single cart leave
The market has had empty stalls for three weeks running, which hasn't happened since the last bad winter
A factor is offering premium rates for anyone willing to haul goods along the old forest road
The vinegar maker has been buying up all the spoiled wine in the area, which usually means he's expecting a shortage of good stock
A shipment of glass arrived intact but nobody placed the order, and the shipper's manifest is for a settlement that doesn't exist
The price of good boots has gone up because the cobbler can't get sole leather at the old rate
A gem trader passing through said the market in the capital has dried up for a particular stone and nobody knows why
The horse trader is turning down offers for his best animals, says he's holding them for a specific buyer who hasn't arrived yet
Someone hired every available porter in the settlement for a single day and wouldn't say what they were moving
The beeswax supply is tighter than anyone expected and the chandler is blaming the cold spring
A merchant from the south showed up with goods that are usually only available from the north, at northern prices
The hay merchant says he's been offered a contract to supply a buyer he's never dealt with, at a location he's never heard of
The tailor has a backlog of orders for heavy-duty clothing: travel cloaks, reinforced packs, thick-soled boots
A trader who usually deals in luxury goods has switched entirely to provisions and won't explain the change
The ore samples from the new vein are being sent to a buyer outside the region instead of the local smelter
A merchant paid a premium to have his goods stored under lock rather than in the open warehouse
The potters' guild raised prices uniformly on the same day, and the merchants suspect coordination
A supplier who used to deal with several local shops is now exclusively serving one buyer and turning everyone else away
The price of writing ink tripled and the scribe says it's because the materials come from somewhere that's no longer accessible
A freight agent has been quietly hiring extra wagons for a route that normally doesn't need them
The woodworker is declining furniture commissions to focus on something he won't show anyone
Someone flooded the market with cheap copper rivets and the local metalworker can't compete
The fish trade is all going through one broker now, and the independent fishermen are being squeezed out
A merchant was caught watering down oil and claimed everyone does it now because the supply is that thin
The last two caravans came in by different routes than usual and the drivers looked relieved to have arrived
A foreign trade delegation passed through without stopping, which is unusual because they always resupply here
The basket weaver says willow supply is down because the creek bank where she cuts was claimed by someone
A carter is charging a danger fee for the north road and won't go without an armed companion
The soap maker ran out of lye and says the ashwood she uses isn't available at any price right now
Someone left a crate of finished goods at the market overnight with a note saying 'no charge,' and nobody knows who
The grain factor has started requiring payment in advance instead of on delivery, which he's never done before
A trader says the bridge toll on the main trade route went up without any notice or posted authority
The dockmaster says river traffic is down by half this season and he doesn't know where the boats are going instead
A traveling metalsmith offered to buy every broken tool in the settlement at scrap weight, which is oddly generous
The local market day has been drawing fewer sellers from outside the settlement each week
The furrier says pelts that used to come from the trappers in the north have stopped entirely
A buyer from a settlement nobody trades with showed up asking for bulk quantities of food staples
The parchment maker raised prices and the clerk says it's because cattle hides are all going to the armorer instead
Someone tried to corner the market on lamp wicks and the shopkeepers are splitting their remaining stock
A merchant confided that three of her regular trade contacts have gone silent in the last month
The surplus grain from last year's harvest is almost gone, and the new crop isn't ready for another two months
A jeweler arrived and set up without the usual market license: when questioned, he produced papers from an authority no one here answers to
The smith's apprentice says the master has been turning raw iron into finished goods at a pace that doesn't match any local orders
A merchant's credit was refused at the market for the first time, and the reasons weren't made public
The dried fruit supply from the western orchards didn't arrive this season and no substitute has been found
A warehouse that's been empty for a year was leased overnight and loaded with unmarked crates within the week
The price of torches has gone up because the pitch supplier says his source is contested by two settlements
A buyer offered to purchase the entire output of the tannery for the next six months at an above-market rate
The road markers on the trade route were painted over with a symbol nobody recognized, and the merchants are uneasy
A courier arrived with sealed letters for three different merchants, and all three packed up and left the next day
The alehouse owner says his best supplier brews for someone else now, exclusively, and the contract appeared out of nowhere
A lumber buyer from outside the region offered to buy green wood, unseasoned, which nobody does unless they're in a hurry
The settlement's trade surplus has shrunk to nothing this quarter and the council hasn't addressed it publicly
A merchant who deals in maps and charts says demand has quadrupled in the last month from people he doesn't usually sell to
The oil press has been running at full capacity and the output is going straight into sealed barrels marked for shipment
A tinker says his usual suppliers are all quoting longer delivery times and higher prices, without explanation
The herbalist bought out the apothecary's entire stock of a particular root and neither will say what it's for
A peddler says the road between here and the eastern settlement used to have three inns along it: two have closed
The price of arrows went up and the fletcher says it's not his doing: the shaft wood is being diverted elsewhere
Someone's been offering well above market rate for any map or document that mentions the old trade routes through the mountains
The merchant guild called an unscheduled meeting and non-members were asked to leave the hall
A caravan master said he'll only take the southern route from now on, even though it adds four days to the trip
The stockyard is fuller than usual but nobody is buying: the sellers are holding and the buyers are waiting
The last shipment of medicine from the coast arrived with half the vials empty, and the supplier claims they were full when shipped
A factor is quietly buying up debt notes from local merchants, which usually means someone is positioning for a buyout
The tallow supply dried up because the rendering house sold its entire output to a single buyer before anyone else could bid
A traveling merchant says the market two settlements over has collapsed completely: stalls empty, no buyers, no sellers
The innkeeper is stockpiling food and drink beyond what normal business warrants, says she'd rather be ready than sorry
The wheelwright has a waiting list for the first time in years: everyone seems to need carts repaired at once
A silver coin showed up in the market that doesn't match any known mint, and the money changer is keeping quiet about where it came from
A broker is paying a premium for any information about road conditions on routes he doesn't usually trade
The stone quarry raised its rates and the builders say the timing suggests coordination with the lumber merchants
Someone is importing finished goods that used to be made locally, and the local craftspeople are losing business
A trade license was revoked and the merchant in question says it's political retaliation, not a legitimate violation
The moneylender raised interest rates and the small traders say they'll be forced out of business within the season
A shipping crate washed up on the riverbank with goods that aren't traded in this region, addressed to no one
The seasonal caravan from the highlands is three weeks late and the goods it carries are already being sold at premium by people who had advance stock
A merchant quietly sold her shop and property last week and nobody knows where she went or why she left in such a hurry
The salt price doubled overnight and the only explanation anyone got was a shrug from the supplier
A timber buyer showed up offering prices nobody could refuse, cleaned out the woodyard, and left before anyone asked where it was going
The cooper can't get iron hoops at any price and says every smith in the region is prioritizing someone else's order
A factor from a settlement we don't trade with started asking around for anyone who knows the old mountain routes
The seed merchant says she's been allocated half her usual stock and the rest is going to settlements she's never supplied before
Someone's been forging market tokens and the stall keepers have stopped accepting them, which is slowing everything down
The mead supply dried up because the beekeeper sold his entire season's honey to a single buyer and won't say who
A caravan arrived with goods wrapped in unfamiliar cloth and markings nobody in the settlement can read
The cordwainer says sole leather is three times what it was last year and the quality has dropped as well
A merchant who usually deals in grain switched to selling preserved meats and won't explain the pivot
The farrier has a line out the door because every traveler coming through needs shoes replaced, as if the roads have gotten harder on iron
Someone offered the innkeeper a year's rent in advance for exclusive use of the back room, no questions asked
The thread merchant says her spinners are being poached by a buyer who pays double and operates out of somewhere nobody's been
A wagon of goods arrived sealed with wax stamps from an authority nobody here recognizes or answers to

The parchment supply dried up because the tanner allocated all his vellum-grade hides to a contract he won't discuss
A merchant is offering free cartage to anyone willing to ship goods east, and the generosity has people suspicious
The market square has had more guards posted than usual and the council hasn't explained why
A trader's warehouse was broken into but nothing was taken: just searched, thoroughly and methodically
The flour price held steady while grain prices climbed, which means the miller is operating at a loss and nobody knows why he'd do that
A merchant from the east arrived with an empty cart, bought nothing, asked questions about production capacity, and left
The soapmaker has switched suppliers three times in a month because each one stopped delivering without notice
A courier delivered a sealed manifest to the market overseer and the overseer closed the market an hour early after reading it
The sawmill doubled its output last month but the lumber isn't being sold locally: it's being shipped out at night
Someone is buying every bit of sulfur and saltpeter the apothecary can get, which has implications nobody wants to discuss

A traveling moneylender set up in the tavern and has been offering loans at rates that seem too good to be legitimate

The candle supply is short because the tallow is being diverted to waterproofing compound and the buyer won't say what for

Someone is quietly buying up all the old maps and survey records that come through the market, paying well above their practical value
A merchant's scales were independently verified and found accurate, but the weights he uses tested light by a consistent margin
The shipment of medicinal bark was redirected en route and arrived at a different settlement than intended, and nobody will take responsibility
The flax crop was good but the linen isn't appearing at market because the weavers are all committed to a single buyer

The settlement's trade balance has tipped negative for the first time in years and the council is meeting behind closed doors about it

Someone brought in a crate of foreign-minted coins and tried to exchange them at face value, but the metal content was half what it should be

The market has been getting quieter each week, not fewer goods but fewer buyers: people are holding onto their coin
